channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Strategies and Challenges of Women Entrepreneurs in Ghana’s Informal Economy
item.description: For many women in Ghana, the informal economy na the main way dem dey make ends meet. Dis economy dey outside formal rules, meaning say plenty people dey trade without official registration, social pr...

item.title: Two Injured in Shooting at Old Dominion University; Gunman Dead
item.description: A shooting occurred Thursday morning at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, resulting in two individuals being critically injured and the gunman dead. The incident took place in the universi...

item.title: Family seeks answers after abduction of Eastleigh hotel owner Abdi Aidid
item.description: The story focuses on the distress of the family of Nairobi businessman Abdi Aidid, who disappeared after being abducted by armed men in the Eastleigh–Shauri Moyo area. Relatives explain that he was a ...

item.title: Childhood Memories of Colourful Antiseptics and Nostalgic Anecdotes
item.description: The article is a lighthearted collection of readers' reflections on childhood experiences with colourful antiseptics like Mercurochrome, Acriflavine, and Gentian Violet. Warren Mitchell recalls the ri...

item.title: Bayelsa State to Host Inaugural Yenagoa 10KM Marathon with Festival-Style Celebration
item.description: The maiden edition of the Yenagoa City International 10KM Race don set, and e dey aimed at promoting Bayelsa State as a global sports tourism destination. The Bayelsa State Government announce say the...

item.title: Business Council Considered Changing Copyright Rules to Let AI Train on Creative Works Without Payment
item.description: In late 2025, the Albanese government firmly ruled out altering copyright law to allow AI companies to use Australian creative works without the creators’ permission or compensation. Despite this, the...

item.title: DHSUD coordinates with housing and real estate developers to address economic impact of Middle East conflict
item.description: The Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development (DHSUD) has announced that it will engage in discussions with private housing developers to prepare measures mitigating the economic impact of...

item.title: British Tourist Faces Cyber-Crime Charges in Dubai Over Video of Iranian Missile Strikes
item.description: A British man from London has been charged under cyber-crime laws in Dubai after allegedly filming Iranian missiles over the city. He is among 21 individuals reportedly facing similar charges, accordi...

item.title: Police Officer Injured in Grenade Attack on Bajaur Police Post
item.description: In Bajaur’s Khar tehsil, a police officer sustained minor injuries after unidentified attackers hurled a hand grenade at a police post near the Jinnah Bus Terminal, which is around three kilometres fr...

item.title: Indian Missions in GCC Facilitate Transit Visas to Help Citizens Return Home During West Asia Conflict
item.description: With the ongoing escalation of the Israel-Iran war, Indian missions in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries — including Qatar, Bahrain, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Oman — are working tirele...

item.title: Opposition Halts FC Upgrade Bill in National Assembly Due to Quorum Issues
item.description: On Thursday, the National Assembly witnessed a halt in the legislative process when the opposition blocked a bill intended to transform the Frontier Constabulary (FC) into a Federal Constabulary. The ...

item.title: S&P cautious on sovereign rating cuts amid Middle East conflict
item.description: S&P Global has indicated that it will not immediately lower sovereign credit ratings following the outbreak of war in the Middle East. During a recent webinar, the firm highlighted that escalating oil...

item.title: Safari Rally Kenya 2026 kicks off in Naivasha with public shakedown and lively local festivities
item.description: Day one of the 2026 Safari Rally Kenya, the third round of the World Rally Championship, saw Naivasha town in Nakuru County transform into a hub of high-octane motorsport action. The rally began with ...

item.title: René Redzepi Resigns from Noma Amid Allegations of Past Physical and Psychological Abuse
item.description: René Redzepi, the founder and head chef of the renowned three-Michelin-star restaurant Noma in Copenhagen, has resigned following renewed allegations of workplace bullying and abuse. The claims, detai...

item.title: Iran Conflict Affects LPG Supply, Indian Families Adjust Wedding Plans Accordingly
item.description: The ongoing tension in Iran, particularly around the Hormuz region, is impacting LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) supplies to India. Families preparing for weddings are facing a dilemma: either they secu...

item.title: Three Spotted Gully Sharks Returned to Eastern Cape Waters for Research
item.description: Three spotted gully sharks that had outgrown the Two Oceans Aquarium’s Kelp Forest Exhibit have been released back into the wild off the Eastern Cape coast, near Mgwalana. The release forms part of a ...

item.title: Investigation underway after two deaths linked to plasma donation in Winnipeg
item.description: Authorities in Winnipeg are investigating the deaths of two individuals who recently donated plasma at a local clinic. The incidents have raised concerns about the safety protocols and medical oversig...

item.title: Kelly Clarkson Reveals She Never Received Her American Idol Season 1 Prizes
item.description: Kelly Clarkson, winner of the inaugural season of American Idol in 2002, has revealed that she never received the prizes promised to her for winning the show. Speaking on The Kelly Clarkson Show with ...

item.title: House passes bill setting BARMM parliamentary elections in September 2026
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Presidential Peace Adviser Carlito Galvez Jr. expressed his appreciation to the House of Representatives for approving the bill that resets the first regular parliamentary elections in...

item.title: How Afrobeats Music Reflects Cybercrime Culture and Its Global Reach
item.description: Dis article explain how Afrobeats, the popular music from Nigeria and West Africa, dey sometimes glamorize cybercrime. Researchers study 40 songs from 2023 to 2025 and find say some lyrics dey praise ...
